content.outcomes_html: Define voltage, current, and resistance and apply V = I x R to simple circuits.
content.explain_html: Use one worked example for each variable rearrangement. Highlight units and decimal conversion.
content.practice_html:
• Calculate current from voltage and resistance.
  • Calculate resistance from voltage and current.
content.assessment_html: Exit ticket: 3 mixed Ohm's Law questions with one real-world appliance context.

content.outcomes_html: Explain current flow and total resistance in series circuits.
content.explain_html: Demonstrate that current is constant in a series path and resistance adds across components.
content.practice_html: Build two series circuits and compare measured vs calculated values.

content.outcomes_html: Describe voltage and current behaviour in parallel circuits.
content.explain_html: Show equal branch voltage and current split across unequal branch resistances.
content.practice_html: Construct a two-branch circuit and record branch current readings.
content.assessment_html: Compare two circuit diagrams and justify which has lower equivalent resistance.

content.outcomes_html: Apply Kirchhoff's Current Law and Voltage Law to one-loop and two-loop examples.
content.explain_html: Use node and loop equations with clear sign convention steps.
content.practice_html: Solve two guided problems, then check by summing volt drops around each loop.
content.assessment_html: Independent problem: derive unknown branch current using KCL.

content.outcomes_html: Calculate electrical power and energy usage for simple loads.
content.explain_html: Introduce P = V x I and E = P x t with unit conversions.
content.practice_html: Estimate daily energy use for three classroom devices.
content.assessment_html: Homework: cost comparison using tariff rates and usage profiles.

content.outcomes_html: Use a digital multimeter safely to measure voltage, current, and resistance.
content.explain_html: Demonstrate range selection, probe placement, and correct meter mode.
content.practice_html: Practical rotations measuring five pre-wired test points.
content.assessment_html: Observed checklist: each learner completes one safe measurement sequence.

content.outcomes_html: Identify electrical hazards and describe a safe isolation process.
content.explain_html: Walk through lockout principles, test-before-touch, and PPE minimums.
content.practice_html: Scenario cards: pick safe actions for each workshop situation.
content.assessment_html: 10-question safety check requiring 80 percent pass threshold.

content.outcomes_html: Differentiate MCB, fuse, and RCD operating principles.
content.explain_html: Use fault scenarios to show which protective device should operate first.
content.practice_html: Match circuit faults to appropriate protective devices and ratings.
content.assessment_html: Written response: justify protective selection for a small workshop circuit.

content.outcomes_html: Use a repeatable fault-finding process on low-voltage circuits.
content.explain_html: Teach confirm-symptom, isolate-section, test, and verify-fix workflow.
content.practice_html: Timed diagnostic exercise with one hidden open-circuit and one short-circuit fault.
content.assessment_html: Learners submit fault logs with evidence and final corrective action.

content.outcomes_html: Explain basic switching logic using relays and contact states.
content.explain_html: Compare normally-open and normally-closed contacts in control circuits.
content.practice_html: Wire a start-stop relay circuit and test latching behaviour.
content.assessment_html: Draw and annotate a simple switching circuit from memory.
